title: Run an interactive rebase to reshape commits
description: Reorder, squash, edit, and reword commits on a branch using git rebase -i, verifying the build at the end.
body: Use when asked to tidy a branch's commits before merge. Do not apply it to commits already pulled by others. Actions: identify the base; run git rebase -i <base>; in the todo list set pick, squash, fixup, edit, or reword per commit; resolve any conflicts the rebase surfaces; build and test at the final state; push with --force-with-lease if the branch was already remote. Validation signals include a clean linear history and passing tests at HEAD. Risk boundary: rewriting published commits is destructive, so confine to private branches or confirm with collaborators first.
